title: Cross-Region Replication for a Relational DB
description:
## Low-Lag Cross-Region Reads with Fast Failover

A company runs a **relational** workload on Amazon Aurora (MySQL-compatible) in one Region. They need to serve **low-latency reads from a second Region** for users there, keep cross-Region replication lag **below one second**, and be able to **promote the second Region to primary quickly** (in roughly a minute) if the primary Region becomes impaired. They want a managed solution purpose-built for this, not a custom replication pipeline.

Which approach BEST meets these requirements?

options.text: Use an Aurora Global Database with the secondary Region as a read endpoint and managed planned/unplanned failover
Schedule periodic cross-Region Aurora snapshot copies and restore them in the second Region when needed
Replace Aurora with DynamoDB Global Tables for multi-Region active-active access
Create a same-Region Aurora read replica and route the second Region's users to it over the internet

explanation: **Aurora Global Database** is purpose-built for exactly this: it uses storage-level replication to a secondary Region with **typically sub-second lag**, serves **low-latency local reads** in the secondary Region, and supports **managed cross-Region failover** that promotes the secondary to a full read/write primary in about a minute. **Cross-Region snapshot copies** (B) give an RPO measured in hours and a slow restore — far from sub-second lag or fast failover. **DynamoDB Global Tables** (C) provide multi-Region active-active, but the workload is **relational**; switching to a NoSQL key-value store changes the data model and is not a drop-in for an Aurora relational app. A **same-Region read replica** (D) does nothing for cross-Region latency or regional failover and forces remote users to read across Regions over the internet.

hints.title: Sub-second + relational
hints.body: The data is relational and the lag target is <1s. Which managed feature is designed for sub-second cross-Region replication of Aurora?

hints.title: Snapshots vs streaming replication
hints.body: Periodic snapshot copies imply an RPO of hours. "Below one second" demands continuous streaming replication.
